rubric.target_patient: Adult women (and rarely men) newly diagnosed with locally advanced breast cancer (Stage II/III, T4 any N M0, inflammatory, or regional Stage IV with only supraclavicular nodal spread) who have received no prior chemotherapy or ipsilateral radiation, have measurable disease ≥2.5cm, and whose tumor is being molecularly profiled (MammaPrint, ER, HER2/TargetPrint) at trial entry to assign them adaptively to one of several neoadjuvant experimental-plus-standard-chemotherapy arms ahead of definitive breast surgery.
rubric.site_burden: No formal Schedule of Assessments was provided, so burden is inferred from the adaptive platform design and interventions: this requires an academic breast oncology infrastructure capable of pre-treatment core biopsy for biomarker assay (MammaPrint/ER/HER2/TargetPrint), serial breast MRI for response prediction, coordination of a 12-week weekly paclitaxel backbone followed by anthracycline-based therapy (weeks 13-16) prior to surgery, multi-arm IV drug administration and toxicity monitoring across several experimental agents (some with cardiac and infusion-reaction risk profiles, e.g. trastuzumab/pertuzumab combinations), tissue/blood banking logistics for correlative science, close coordination with breast surgery for post-neoadjuvant resection and pCR determination, and participation in a Bayesian adaptive randomization scheme requiring rapid, accurate biomarker reporting back to a central statistical hub. This is substantially more operationally complex than a fixed two-arm neoadjuvant trial.

criteria.requirement: Investigator must be a medical oncologist (or breast surgical/radiation oncologist functioning as PI) with a breast cancer-focused practice, not a general oncologist without breast subspecialization.
criteria.satisfies_if: NPPES taxonomy lists Hematology/Oncology, Medical Oncology, or Surgical Oncology with a documented breast cancer focus (publication or trial history), or self-attestation of a dedicated breast oncology practice.
criteria.derived_from: Conditions list (Breast Neoplasms, TNBC, HER2+/HER2- BC) and neoadjuvant chemotherapy design targeting breast cancer subtypes

criteria.requirement: Investigator should have documented experience managing neoadjuvant chemotherapy regimens (anthracycline/taxane-based) in breast cancer prior to surgery.
criteria.satisfies_if: PubMed record includes at least one publication on neoadjuvant breast cancer chemotherapy, or investigator has served as PI/sub-I on a neoadjuvant breast cancer trial per ClinicalTrials.gov.
criteria.derived_from: Standard Therapy arm: paclitaxel weeks 1-12, doxorubicin/cyclophosphamide weeks 13-16 pre-surgery

criteria.requirement: Investigator should understand molecular subtyping tools (MammaPrint, IHC/FISH HER2, TargetPrint) used to assign adaptive treatment arms.
criteria.satisfies_if: Publication record or self-report indicates familiarity with genomic assay-based breast cancer stratification (e.g., MammaPrint, HER2 testing methods).
criteria.derived_from: Tumor assay profile eligibility requiring MammaPrint High/Low, ER, HER2 status via IHC/FISH/TargetPrint

criteria.requirement: Practice must regularly see and treat women with newly diagnosed Stage II-III or T4/inflammatory breast cancer eligible for neoadjuvant therapy, in sufficient volume to contribute to a 5000-patient multi-site adaptive platform.
criteria.satisfies_if: Medicare claims or self-report indicate a baseline volume of newly diagnosed locally advanced (Stage II-III/T4/inflammatory) breast cancer patients seen annually consistent with an active breast oncology referral practice.
criteria.derived_from: Eligible tumors: Stage II or III, T4 any N M0, inflammatory or regional Stage IV with supraclavicular-only mets

criteria.requirement: Site must have access to chemotherapy- and radiation-naive breast cancer patients at first diagnosis, prior to any systemic treatment.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms the practice typically enrolls patients at initial diagnosis prior to any cytotoxic or radiation therapy, consistent with a primary breast cancer referral or tumor board pathway.
criteria.derived_from: No prior cytotoxic regimens or ipsilateral radiation therapy allowed for this malignancy

criteria.requirement: Practice must see breast cancer patients across the full receptor/HER2 spectrum (TNBC, HR+/HER2-, HER2+) to populate the trial's multiple biomarker signature arms.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report or practice profile confirms routine treatment of TNBC, HER2-positive, and hormone receptor-positive breast cancer patients, not a single-subtype-limited practice.
criteria.derived_from: Conditions list spanning TNBC, HER2-positive, HER2-negative, HR-positive, HR-negative tumors

criteria.requirement: Site must have on-site or closely coordinated access to serial breast MRI imaging and image-guided core biopsy for baseline and on-treatment biomarker assessment.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms institutional access to breast MRI and core needle biopsy services integrated with the research team's workflow.
criteria.derived_from: Willingness to undergo core biopsy of primary lesion and imaging-based response prediction central to trial design

criteria.requirement: Site must be able to administer and monitor multiple concurrent/sequential IV investigational and standard agents (paclitaxel, doxorubicin/cyclophosphamide, trastuzumab, pertuzumab, and other targeted agents) with associated toxicity management (e.g., cardiac monitoring for HER2-targeted therapy).
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms an infusion center with capacity for multi-agent IV chemotherapy/biologic administration and cardiac monitoring (echocardiogram/MUGA access) for HER2-targeted regimens.
criteria.derived_from: Interventions list including Paclitaxel, Doxorubicin/Cyclophosphamide, Trastuzumab, Pertuzumab, T-DM1, and other closed experimental arms requiring IV infusion infrastructure

criteria.requirement: Site must coordinate neoadjuvant treatment completion with breast surgical resection for pathologic complete response assessment.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms established referral or co-located relationship with breast surgical oncology for post-neoadjuvant surgery and pathology review.
criteria.derived_from: Primary outcome: pathologic complete response (pCR) assessed post-surgery

criteria.requirement: Investigator should have prior experience as PI or sub-investigator on interventional oncology (ideally breast cancer neoadjuvant) trials, given the complexity of this adaptive multi-arm platform.
criteria.satisfies_if: ClinicalTrials.gov record lists the physician as PI or sub-I on at least one completed or active breast cancer interventional trial.
criteria.derived_from: Phase 2 adaptive multi-arm platform trial design (I-SPY 2) with rotating experimental arms

criteria.requirement: Investigator should have a track record of industry-sponsored research funding/payments consistent with active clinical trial conduct, given the multiple pharma-supplied agents in this platform.
criteria.satisfies_if: Open Payments database shows research payment records to the investigator or affiliated institution from oncology drug sponsors.
criteria.derived_from: Multiple sponsor drug arms (AMG 386, AMG 479, MK-2206, T-DM1, Pertuzumab, Ganetespib, ABT-888, Neratinib, PLX3397, Pembrolizumab) indicating a pharma-collaborative, multi-sponsor platform

excluded_screening: ECOG performance status 0-1 — per-patient fitness check, not a physician-fit signal
Willingness to undergo core biopsy — a per-patient consent/willingness item, not a site capability (site MRI/biopsy infrastructure is captured separately)
Non-pregnant and non-lactating — per-patient eligibility, irrelevant to investigator qualification
Ability to understand and sign informed consent (Screening Consent) — per-patient consent capacity
Normal organ and marrow function labs (leukocytes, ANC, platelets, bilirubin, AST/ALT, creatinine) — per-patient lab thresholds checked at enrollment
No uncontrolled cardiac disease / baseline EF ≥50% — per-patient cardiac fitness screening
No clinical/imaging evidence of distant metastases (CXR, bone scan, LFTs) — per-patient staging workup, not a site-level requirement
Ability to understand and sign I-SPY 2 Consent #2 — per-patient consent capacity
